title: Office Conversation
definition: Common phrases used in everyday office interactions with colleagues and managers.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: Do you have a minute to discuss the client update?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: Sure, give me two minutes to finish this email.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: No problem. Can you also share the status report by end of day?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: Yes, I'll send it before 5 PM.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: Great, thanks for the quick turnaround.

practicePhrases: Could you please share the update by EOD?
I'll get back to you on this by tomorrow.
Let's schedule a quick call to discuss this.
I'm currently tied up with something urgent.
Can we push this meeting to 3 PM?
Thanks for your patience, I'll follow up shortly.

commonMistakes.right: Please reply/get back to me on this.
commonMistakes.why: 'Revert back' is redundant; 'revert' alone historically means 'go back', so just say 'reply' or 'get back to'.

commonMistakes.right: I have a doubt / I have a question.
commonMistakes.why: 'Have' (not the continuous form) is used with 'doubt' as a possession, not an ongoing action.

quiz.question: Which phrase politely asks for more time?
quiz.explanation: This politely requests a short delay.

quiz.sentence: I will revert back to you by evening.
quiz.correction: I will get back to you by evening.
quiz.explanation: Avoid the redundant phrase 'revert back'; use 'get back to' instead.
